- KPI Snapshot
- Dashboard Definition
- Dashboard Widget
- Custom Field Definition

### Naming strategy

The naming rules established:

- use singular entity names
- use UUIDs as IDs
- use clear human-readable code fields where needed:
	- site\_code
		- batch\_code
		- task\_code
		- cohort\_code
		- dispatch\_number
		- order\_number

### High-risk areas to control early

1. Location model must stay generic, not greenhouse-only.
2. Batch model must stay universal across crops, fish, packhouse, etc.
3. Workflow model must attach to entity types and operations, not pages.
4. Dashboard model must use widget + layout + permission model.
5. Permissions must exist in backend action layer, not only frontend hiding.
6. Customization must use custom fields/config/versioned workflows, not direct schema mutation.

### Option A vs Option B decision

The user asked which was better for quality:

- **Option A:** build Terra Schema Package v1 first
- **Option B:** build first 30 actual database tables immediately  
	Decision made:
- **Option A is better for quality**
- **Option B is faster**
- Best path is **A first, then B**  
	Rationale:
- A fixes structure, ownership, enums, relationships, and migration order first.
- B before A increases risk of:
	- duplicated entities
		- weak foreign keys
		- inconsistent naming
		- confused workflow ownership
		- broken traceability
		- wrong dependency order
		- schema drift

### What starts after A

The post-A sequence decided:

1. Freeze Schema Package v1 as source of truth.
2. Generate ERD and migration map.
3. Build first foundation tables only:
	- organization
		- organization\_settings
		- user
		- organization\_membership
		- role
		- permission
		- user\_role\_assignment
		- site
		- location\_type
		- location\_node
4. Build operational backbone:
	- production\_system\_type
		- production\_unit
		- species
		- variety
		- life\_stage
		- asset\_category
		- asset
		- workflow\_definition
		- workflow\_version
		- task\_template
		- task\_instance
		- audit\_log
5. Then define action-based API contracts.
6. Then build first backend modules:
	- tenant/identity
		- location
		- permission
		- workflow
		- task execution
7. Then first UI:
	- login
		- org/site selector
		- admin user management
		- role/permission screen
		- location tree viewer
		- workflow definition screen
		- my tasks screen

### Build packages after A

#### Build Package 01 — Core Foundation

- database tables for tenant, identity, site, location
- migrations
- seed data
- permission checks
- audit logging hooks
- tests

#### Build Package 02 — Workflow Core

- workflow\_definition
- workflow\_version
- task\_template
- task\_instance
- task\_event
- execution rules
- tests

#### Build Package 03 — Worker Operations MVP

- my tasks
- task assignment
- task completion
- location-guided work
- supervisor reassignment
- incident flagging

### Terra Schema Package v1

A full implementation-format schema package was then created.

#### Module ownership map

Modules and owned tables were defined:

- Module A — Tenant & Identity
- Module B — Location & Production Structure
- Module C — Biology & Traceability
- Module D — Workflow & Task Engine
- Module E — Assets & Maintenance
- Module F — Measurements & Alerts
- Module G — Inventory & Procurement
- Module H — Packhouse & Commerce
- Module I — SOP, Training & Compliance
- Module J — Analytics & Configuration
- Cross-cutting module — audit\_log

#### Global schema rules

- Most tables should include:
	- id UUID PK
		- organization\_id where applicable
		- created\_at
		- updated\_at
- Use UUID PKs on all business tables.
- Do not soft delete everything; prefer business `status`.
- Never hard delete traceability or audit records.
- All tenant-owned records must include `organization_id`.
- Operational records should include `site_id`.
- Work/measurements/assets/production should include `location_node_id` where meaningful.
- Human-readable codes must be unique within correct scope.
- Writes to critical entities must create audit log events.
- Use DB enums only for highly stable values.
- Use reference tables where values may expand.

#### Stable enums defined

The following stable enums were specified:

- organization\_status
- membership\_status
- role\_type
- production\_unit\_status
- lifecycle\_status
- task\_status
- result\_status
- asset\_status
- criticality\_level
- measurement\_quality\_flag
- alert\_severity
- incident\_status
- stock\_transaction\_type
- purchase\_request\_status
- purchase\_order\_status
- sales\_order\_status
- dispatch\_status
- document\_status

#### Reference tables explicitly not to be enums

- location\_type
- production\_system\_type
- species
- variety
- life\_stage
- asset\_category
- sensor\_type
- item\_category
- permission

#### Full table-level schema package

A strict field-level definition was produced for **72 entities/tables**, including:

- field names
- PK/FK expectations
- uniqueness constraints
- indexes
- nullable vs required logic
- module ownership
- some dependency notes

#### Relationship rules

- Required roots:
	- organization
		- app\_user
		- organization\_membership
		- site
		- location\_node
- Operational tables should link to organization/site/location/responsible membership as appropriate.
- Limited polymorphic references allowed only where necessary:
	- source\_type + source\_reference\_id
		- linked\_entity\_type + linked\_entity\_id
		- reference\_type + reference\_id
		- task\_source\_type + task\_source\_id
- Self-referencing allowed for:
	- location\_node
		- biological\_batch
		- asset\_category

#### Index strategy

High-priority indexes required:

- all FK columns
- task\_instance(status, assigned\_to\_membership\_id)
- measurement\_record(measurement\_type\_code, measured\_at)
- stock\_transaction(reference\_type, reference\_id)
- audit\_log(entity\_type, entity\_id, timestamp)
- location\_node(site\_id, parent\_location\_node\_id)

Likely search indexes:

- app\_user(email)
- site(code)
- asset(asset\_tag)
- item\_master(sku)
- sales\_order(order\_number)
- biological\_batch(code)
- animal\_cohort(cohort\_code)

#### Migration order

A 9-wave migration order was defined covering all 72 schema objects in the following wave structure:

- Wave 1 — Foundation
- Wave 2 — Structure and master references
- Wave 3 — Workflow core
- Wave 4 — Biology and operations
- Wave 5 — Assets and field events
- Wave 6 — Inventory and procurement
- Wave 7 — Packhouse and commerce
- Wave 8 — SOP and compliance
- Wave 9 — Analytics and configuration

A dependency issue was explicitly noted:

- `task_template.required_sop_id` depends on `sop_document`
- Best quality path: add `required_sop_id` later via alter migration

#### Seed data order

- Seed 1:
	- permission
		- system roles
		- location\_type
		- production\_system\_type
		- species
		- life\_stage
		- asset\_category
		- sensor\_type
		- item\_category
- Seed 2:
	- demo organization
		- organization settings
		- admin user
		- admin membership
		- role assignments
		- demo site
- Seed 3:
	- demo location tree
		- demo production units
		- demo workflows
		- demo task templates
		- demo shifts
- Seed 4:
	- demo item masters
		- demo suppliers
		- demo customers
		- demo dashboards
		- demo KPI definitions

#### Backend action ownership

Action-based backend ownership was specified rather than raw CRUD:

- Tenant & Identity:
	- invite\_member
		- activate\_member
		- suspend\_member
		- assign\_role
		- revoke\_role
- Location:
	- create\_site
		- add\_location\_node
		- move\_location\_node
		- deactivate\_location\_node
- Workflow:
	- create\_workflow
		- version\_workflow
		- publish\_workflow
		- simulate\_workflow
- Task:
	- assign\_task
		- start\_task
		- pause\_task
		- complete\_task
		- fail\_task
		- skip\_task
		- reassign\_task
- Inventory:
	- receive\_stock
		- move\_stock
		- reserve\_stock
		- consume\_stock
		- adjust\_stock
		- dispatch\_stock
- Biology:
	- create\_crop\_cycle
		- stock\_cohort
		- transfer\_cohort
		- record\_mortality
		- record\_growth\_sample
		- harvest\_batch

#### Agent constraints

Forbidden:

- duplicate batch concepts
- greenhouse-only hardcoding
- page-led schema
- bypassing organization boundaries
- frontend-only permissions
- writing directly to audit tables from UI
- ad hoc JSON blobs for core relational data

Allowed JSONB uses:

- layout configs
- workflow schemas
- validation rules
- metadata extensions
- tenant custom config

Change control for any new core table after approval:

- purpose
- owning module
- why existing tables are insufficient
- FK plan
- migration placement
- audit/permission impact

### What starts immediately after Schema Package approval

The next recommended deliverable was:

- **Build Package 01 spec**  
	containing:
- exact tables to create
- exact indexes
- seed content
- API endpoints
- tests
- acceptance criteria
- owner agent assignments

## YBA Systems

### YBATerra / Terra architecture direction

- Terra is being designed as a major system under the broader YBA ecosystem.
- Terra should be treated as a controlled platform build, not an ad hoc app project.
- The system must support:
	- multi-tenancy
		- configurable workflows
		- customizable dashboards
		- strict role/permission control
		- worker-first operational UI
		- traceability
		- auditability
		- future AI/automation readiness

### YBA system development philosophy established

- Architecture first
- Schema first
- Controlled migrations
- Permissions before operational write logic
- Auditability for critical actions
- Layered execution by agent ownership
- Strong separation of modules
- No uncontrolled page-driven schema design
- No free-form code generation without dependency discipline

### Immediate next YBA systems steps from this conversation

1. Treat Terra Schema Package v1 as the controlled source of truth.
2. Move next into **Build Package 01 — Core Foundation**.
3. After Build Package 01, proceed to:
	- Build Package 02 — Structure + Workflow Core
		- Build Package 03 — Worker Operations MVP
4. Use the agent roster and migration waves to control OpenClaw execution.
5. Preserve this conversation as a future-chat resource because it contains:
	- the build task plan
		- agent roster
		- master data model
		- schema package v1
		- sequencing logic
		- quality-first decisions

- No usernames, passwords, tokens, API keys, or other credentials were discussed in this conversation.
